commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6d05bb66acb3e5c50f7f70e64f77ef36392e8960)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a canonical `title` field across session protocol schemas, durable session metadata, gateway create/list/patch/resolve helpers, `/name` command behavior, Control UI display selection, and focused tests while mirroring legacy `label`.

PR surface: Source +70, Tests -1. Total +69 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings by source inspection: PR head compares raw `title` and `label` before normalized parsing, and the shared Control UI row type remains label-only while the helper casts around it. No live after-fix behavior proof was supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are maintainer acceptance of the title-vs-label contract plus contributor real behavior proof, not a safe standalone automation repair.

target.title: fix(sessions): migrate session naming to canonical title
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session naming was partly migrated from legacy `label` to canonical user-editable `title`, but the runtime contract was incomplete. New gateway schemas accepted `title` while `sessions.create` dropped it, `sessions.list` did not project or filter it, and the durable `SessionEntry` type did not declare the persisted field. That left new title-based clients and older label-based clients able to disagree about the same session name.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the title contract while preserving the legacy label mirror:

- `sessions.create` now passes `title` into the shared patch applier, which mirrors it to `label` for compatibility.
- `SessionEntry` now declares `title?: string` as durable session metadata.
- Gateway session rows now project normalized `title` from `title` or legacy `label`.
- `sessions.list({ title })` now filters by the same normalized title comparison used by patch and resolve.
- Session list search includes the canonical title field.
- Focused tests cover `/name`, create, patch, resolve, list projection/filtering, and legacy label compatibility.

## User Impact

Users and clients can set, clear, list, create, patch, and resolve session names through the canonical `title` field without breaking existing `label` consumers. Legacy label-only session entries still appear as titled rows and remain resolvable through title-based lookup.

## Evidence

Head SHA: `6d05bb66acb3e5c50f7f70e64f77ef36392e8960`

Focused local behavior proof:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  14 passed (14)
Tests  706 passed (706)
Duration  56.58s

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed (7)
Duration  34.18s

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 107.95s
```

Additional local checks:

```bash
git diff --check
```

Result: passed with no whitespace errors.

GitHub Actions on the previous head already had `Workflow Sanity` passing; the new head should rerun the proof gate against this authored evidence section.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
